name: ISA-99
description: Legacy industrial network standard establishing basic zoning security concepts.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Reference Model Architecture
questions.text: Is the logical network structure mapped to the zones and conduits reference model?
questions.description:
Verify comprehensive network drawings, zone demarcations, and conduit interface routing maps.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the zoning architecture configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Electronic Security Zones
questions.text: Are logical domains with shared security requirements isolated in separate zones?
questions.description:
Audit switch configs, router interfaces, and edge isolation firewall filtering rules.

3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the zone segregation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Conduit Interface Security
questions.text: Are all communications crossing zone boundaries mediated through defined conduits?

Verify firewall rules, session controls, and proxy mediation parameters on cross-zone routes.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the conduit mediation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Operator Domain Identification
questions.text: Are interactive operators authenticated when navigating between distinct security zones (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Check credentials gates on transit nodes, Active Directory group checks, and MFA tunnels.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the access control boundary configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical ISA 99 LEGACY systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Field Network Segregation
questions.text: Are local process loops completely isolated from corporate business networking traffic?
questions.description:
Ensure zero direct routing exists between office subnetworks and Level 1 PLC segments.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the logical isolation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Boundary Traffic Monitoring
questions.text: Is network traffic passing through conduits monitored in real-time (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Audit intrusion sensor capture logs, flow parameters, and boundary firewall alert queues.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the network auditing configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: ISA-99 - System Configuration Baselines
questions.text: Are baseline configuration parameters documented for all zone boundary firewalls?
questions.description:
Verify configuration version files, change ticket tracking, and administrative review approvals.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the change security controls configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Field Device Port Lockdown
questions.text: Are unused physical and logical ports locked down on cross-zone switches?
questions.description:
Verify physical switch locks, port lockdown status files, and administrative disable settings.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the boundary hardening configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: ISA-99 - System Access Rights
questions.text: Are access permissions to configure conduits restricted to authorized administrators?
questions.description:
Review admin access credentials, check group policies, and audit session connection logs.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the role boundaries configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISA-99 - Network Resiliency Controls
questions.text: Is communication inside process zones protected against network traffic floods?
questions.description:
Verify switch rate-limiting, broadcast storm controls, and loop detection setups.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the system resilience configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.
